texto: One should remember that where nobody flees, there is no pursuer: that where there are no little fish, there can be no big ones. Let her discard all fear, behave nobly and yield not her youth to the weak and faint-hearted.

texto: As God has not made anything useless in this world, as all beings fulfill obligations or a role in the sublime drama of Creation, I cannot exempt from this duty, and small though it be, I too have a mission to fill, as for example: alleviating the sufferings of my fellowmen.

texto: We young Filipinos are trying to make over a nation and must not halt in our march, but from time to time turn our gaze upon our elders. We shall wish to read in their countenances approval of our actions.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Jose Rizal sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “We young Filipinos are trying to make over a nation and must not halt in our march, but from time…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a tempo, wish, trying, útil em momentos de trying e nation.

texto: Our liberty will not be secured at the sword's point... We must secure it by making ourselves worthy of it. And when the people reaches that height, God will provide a weapon, the idols will be shattered, tyranny will crumble like a house of cards, and liberty will shine out like the first dawn.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a José Rizal: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a autoestima. O trecho “Our liberty will not be secured at the sword's point... We must secure it by making ourselves wor…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, freedom, liberty pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de liberty.
